title: Compiling with '--threads 1' causes code to crash near access to t_s.t_contextp in threadContextp()
body:
Location:
```
 703│     /// Return the VerilatedContext for the current thread
 704│     static VerilatedContext* threadContextp() {
 705├───────> if (VL_UNLIKELY(!t_s.t_contextp)) t_s.t_contextp = lastContextp();
 706│         return t_s.t_contextp;
 707│     }
```
because ```t_s.t_contextp``` is ```null```:
```
(gdb) p t_s.t_contextp
$1 = (VerilatedContext *) 0x0
```

The [documentation](https://www.mankier.com/1/verilator#Multithreading) doesn't say that any other changes are required compared to ```--threads 0```.
